name: wgetpaste
title: wgetpaste

commands.name: wgetpaste

arguments.raw: -l, --language LANG           set language (defaults to "Plain Text")

arguments.metavar: - escription DESCRIPTION set description defaults to "stdin" or filename
arguments.raw: -d, --description DESCRIPTION set description (defaults to "stdin" or filename)
-n, --nick NICK               set nick (defaults to your username)
-s, --service SERVICE         set service to use (defaults to "dpaste")

arguments.description: set when it should expire (defaults to "30 days")
arguments.raw: -e, --expiration EXPIRATION   set when it should expire (defaults to "30 days")
-S, --list-services           list supported pastebin services

arguments.description: list languages supported by the specified service
arguments.raw: -L, --list-languages          list languages supported by the specified service

arguments.description: list expiration setting supported by the specified service
arguments.raw: -E, --list-expiration         list expiration setting supported by the specified service
-u, --tinyurl URL             convert input url to tinyurl

arguments.description: paste COMMAND and the output of COMMAND
arguments.raw: -c, --command COMMAND         paste COMMAND and the output of COMMAND
-i, --info                    append the output of `emerge --info`
-I, --info-only               paste the output of `emerge --info` only
-x, --xcut                    read input from clipboard (requires x11-misc/xclip)

arguments.description: write resulting url to the X primary selection buffer (requires x11-misc/xclip)
arguments.raw: -X, --xpaste                  write resulting url to the X primary selection buffer (requires x11-misc/xclip)

arguments.description: write resulting url to the X clipboard selection buffer (requires x11-misc/xclip)
arguments.raw: -C, --xclippaste              write resulting url to the X clipboard selection buffer (requires x11-misc/xclip)

arguments.description: show url for the raw paste (no syntax highlighting or html)
arguments.raw: -r, --raw                     show url for the raw paste (no syntax highlighting or html)

arguments.description: use tee to show what is being pasted
arguments.raw: -t, --tee                     use tee to show what is being pasted

arguments.description: show wget stderr output if no url is received
arguments.raw: -v, --verbose                 show wget stderr output if no url is received

arguments.description: emit output suitable for shell completions (only affects --list-*)
arguments.raw: --completions             emit output suitable for shell completions (only affects --list-*)
--debug                   be *very* verbose (implies -v)
-h, --help                    show this help
-g, --ignore-configs          ignore /etc/wgetpaste.conf, ~/.wgetpaste.conf etc.
--version                 show version information

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# wgetpaste -h
 Usage: /usr/bin/wgetpaste [options] [file[s]]
 
 Options:
   -l, --language LANG           set language (defaults to "Plain Text")
   -d, --description DESCRIPTION set description (defaults to "stdin" or filename)
   -n, --nick NICK               set nick (defaults to your username)
   -s, --service SERVICE         set service to use (defaults to "dpaste")
   -e, --expiration EXPIRATION   set when it should expire (defaults to "30 days")
 
   -S, --list-services           list supported pastebin services
   -L, --list-languages          list languages supported by the specified service
   -E, --list-expiration         list expiration setting supported by the specified service
 
   -u, --tinyurl URL             convert input url to tinyurl
 
   -c, --command COMMAND         paste COMMAND and the output of COMMAND
   -i, --info                    append the output of `emerge --info`
   -I, --info-only               paste the output of `emerge --info` only
   -x, --xcut                    read input from clipboard (requires x11-misc/xclip)
   -X, --xpaste                  write resulting url to the X primary selection buffer (requires x11-misc/xclip)
   -C, --xclippaste              write resulting url to the X clipboard selection buffer (requires x11-misc/xclip)
 
   -r, --raw                     show url for the raw paste (no syntax highlighting or html)
   -t, --tee                     use tee to show what is being pasted
   -v, --verbose                 show wget stderr output if no url is received
       --completions             emit output suitable for shell completions (only affects --list-*)
       --debug                   be *very* verbose (implies -v)
 
   -h, --help                    show this help
   -g, --ignore-configs          ignore /etc/wgetpaste.conf, ~/.wgetpaste.conf etc.
       --version                 show version information
 
 Defaults (DEFAULT_{NICK,LANGUAGE,EXPIRATION}[_${SERVICE}] and DEFAULT_SERVICE)
 can be overridden globally in /etc/wgetpaste.conf or /etc/wgetpaste.d/*.conf or
 per user in any of ~/.wgetpaste.conf or ~/.wgetpaste.d/*.conf.
 
 An additional http header can be passed by setting HEADER_${SERVICE} in any of the
 configuration files mentioned above. For example, authenticating with github gist:
 HEADER_gists="Authorization: token 1234abc56789...", or with gitlab snippets:
 HEADER_snippets="PRIVATE-TOKEN: 1234abc56789..."
 
 You can also set PUBLIC_gists='false' if you want to default to secret instead of
 public github gists. In the case of gitlab, you can set VISIBILITY_snippets= to
 'public', 'private' or 'internal'"
 
 To change your gitlab server, you can override the default API URL setting
 URL_snippets='https://gitlab.[server].com/api/v4/snippets'

local_help.--help:
Usage: /usr/bin/wgetpaste [options] [file[s]]

Options:
    -l, --language LANG           set language (defaults to "Plain Text")
    -d, --description DESCRIPTION set description (defaults to "stdin" or filename)
    -n, --nick NICK               set nick (defaults to your username)
    -s, --service SERVICE         set service to use (defaults to "dpaste")
    -e, --expiration EXPIRATION   set when it should expire (defaults to "30 days")

    -S, --list-services           list supported pastebin services
    -L, --list-languages          list languages supported by the specified service
    -E, --list-expiration         list expiration setting supported by the specified service

    -u, --tinyurl URL             convert input url to tinyurl

    -c, --command COMMAND         paste COMMAND and the output of COMMAND
    -i, --info                    append the output of `emerge --info`
    -I, --info-only               paste the output of `emerge --info` only
    -x, --xcut                    read input from clipboard (requires x11-misc/xclip)
    -X, --xpaste                  write resulting url to the X primary selection buffer (requires x11-misc/xclip)
    -C, --xclippaste              write resulting url to the X clipboard selection buffer (requires x11-misc/xclip)

    -r, --raw                     show url for the raw paste (no syntax highlighting or html)
    -t, --tee                     use tee to show what is being pasted
    -v, --verbose                 show wget stderr output if no url is received
        --completions             emit output suitable for shell completions (only affects --list-*)
        --debug                   be *very* verbose (implies -v)

    -h, --help                    show this help
    -g, --ignore-configs          ignore /etc/wgetpaste.conf, ~/.wgetpaste.conf etc.
        --version                 show version information

Defaults (DEFAULT_{NICK,LANGUAGE,EXPIRATION}[_${SERVICE}] and DEFAULT_SERVICE)
can be overridden globally in /etc/wgetpaste.conf or /etc/wgetpaste.d/*.conf or
per user in any of ~/.wgetpaste.conf or ~/.wgetpaste.d/*.conf.

An additional http header can be passed by setting HEADER_${SERVICE} in any of the
configuration files mentioned above. For example, authenticating with github gist:
HEADER_gists="Authorization: token 1234abc56789...", or with gitlab snippets:
HEADER_snippets="PRIVATE-TOKEN: 1234abc56789..."

You can also set PUBLIC_gists='false' if you want to default to secret instead of
public github gists. In the case of gitlab, you can set VISIBILITY_snippets= to
'public', 'private' or 'internal'"

To change your gitlab server, you can override the default API URL setting
URL_snippets='https://gitlab.[server].com/api/v4/snippets'
local_help.-h:
Usage: /usr/bin/wgetpaste [options] [file[s]]

Options:
    -l, --language LANG           set language (defaults to "Plain Text")
    -d, --description DESCRIPTION set description (defaults to "stdin" or filename)
    -n, --nick NICK               set nick (defaults to your username)
    -s, --service SERVICE         set service to use (defaults to "dpaste")
    -e, --expiration EXPIRATION   set when it should expire (defaults to "30 days")

    -S, --list-services           list supported pastebin services
    -L, --list-languages          list languages supported by the specified service
    -E, --list-expiration         list expiration setting supported by the specified service

    -u, --tinyurl URL             convert input url to tinyurl

    -c, --command COMMAND         paste COMMAND and the output of COMMAND
    -i, --info                    append the output of `emerge --info`
    -I, --info-only               paste the output of `emerge --info` only
    -x, --xcut                    read input from clipboard (requires x11-misc/xclip)
    -X, --xpaste                  write resulting url to the X primary selection buffer (requires x11-misc/xclip)
    -C, --xclippaste              write resulting url to the X clipboard selection buffer (requires x11-misc/xclip)

    -r, --raw                     show url for the raw paste (no syntax highlighting or html)
    -t, --tee                     use tee to show what is being pasted
    -v, --verbose                 show wget stderr output if no url is received
        --completions             emit output suitable for shell completions (only affects --list-*)
        --debug                   be *very* verbose (implies -v)

    -h, --help                    show this help
    -g, --ignore-configs          ignore /etc/wgetpaste.conf, ~/.wgetpaste.conf etc.
        --version                 show version information

Defaults (DEFAULT_{NICK,LANGUAGE,EXPIRATION}[_${SERVICE}] and DEFAULT_SERVICE)
can be overridden globally in /etc/wgetpaste.conf or /etc/wgetpaste.d/*.conf or
per user in any of ~/.wgetpaste.conf or ~/.wgetpaste.d/*.conf.

An additional http header can be passed by setting HEADER_${SERVICE} in any of the
configuration files mentioned above. For example, authenticating with github gist:
HEADER_gists="Authorization: token 1234abc56789...", or with gitlab snippets:

You can also set PUBLIC_gists='false' if you want to default to secret instead of
public github gists. In the case of gitlab, you can set VISIBILITY_snippets= to
'public', 'private' or 'internal'"

To change your gitlab server, you can override the default API URL setting
URL_snippets='https://gitlab.[server].com/api/v4/snippets'

binary_metadata.file_type: Bourne-Again shell script, Unicode text, UTF-8 text executable

apt_metadata.description:
Command-line interface to various online pastebin services
This package contains a script that automates pasting to a number of pastebin
services.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use wgetpaste because ### wgetpaste.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `/usr/bin/wgetpaste [options] [file[s]]` as documented upstream for wgetpaste.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair wgetpaste with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `wgetpaste --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: ### wgetpaste.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install wgetpaste
